questions.q: Which leader does the Statue of Unity depict?
questions.explanation: The Statue of Unity portrays Sardar Vallabhbhai Patel, India's first Deputy Prime Minister and Home Minister.

questions.q: What is the Statue of Unity famous for being?
options: The heaviest building in the world
questions.explanation: At 182 metres, the Statue of Unity is the world's tallest statue.

questions.q: In which Indian state is the Statue of Unity located?
questions.explanation: The Statue of Unity stands in the state of Gujarat in western India.

questions.q: Approximately how tall is the Statue of Unity?
questions.explanation: The Statue of Unity rises to a height of 182 metres.

questions.q: On the bank of which river does the Statue of Unity stand?
questions.explanation: The statue overlooks the Narmada River in Gujarat, near the Sardar Sarovar Dam.

questions.q: In which year was the Statue of Unity unveiled?
questions.explanation: The Statue of Unity was inaugurated on 31 October 2018.

questions.q: Sardar Patel, whom the statue honours, is popularly remembered as the of India.
questions.explanation: Vallabhbhai Patel is widely known as the 'Iron Man of India' for unifying the princely states.

questions.q: What does the name 'Statue of Unity' chiefly commemorate about Sardar Patel?
options: His role in writing the Constitution
His unification of India's princely states
His leadership of the Indian Army
questions.explanation: The name reflects Patel's role in integrating over 500 princely states into a unified India after independence.

questions.q: Which Indian Prime Minister inaugurated the Statue of Unity in 2018?
questions.explanation: Prime Minister Narendra Modi unveiled the statue on 31 October 2018, Patel's birth anniversary.

questions.q: Near which major dam is the Statue of Unity situated?
questions.explanation: The statue stands a few kilometres downstream of the Sardar Sarovar Dam on the Narmada River.

questions.q: On which river islet/hillock in the Narmada does the Statue of Unity itself stand?
questions.explanation: The statue is built on the Sadhu Bet hillock, a rocky islet in the Narmada connected to the mainland by a bridge, and it faces the Sardar Sarovar Dam.

questions.q: Who was the lead sculptor of the Statue of Unity?
questions.explanation: The renowned Indian sculptor Ram V. Sutar designed and led the sculpting of the Statue of Unity.

questions.q: The outer skin of the Statue of Unity is largely clad in which material?
questions.explanation: The statue's exterior is covered with bronze cladding panels over a steel and reinforced-concrete core.

questions.q: Which Indian construction company built the Statue of Unity?
questions.explanation: Larsen & Toubro (L&T) was the engineering and construction firm that executed the project.

questions.q: On which date each year, marking Patel's birthday, was the statue inaugurated and is celebrated?
questions.explanation: Patel was born on 31 October 1875, observed as Rashtriya Ekta Diwas (National Unity Day).

questions.q: What national observance, instituted to honour Patel, falls on the statue's inauguration date?
questions.explanation: 31 October is celebrated as Rashtriya Ekta Diwas, the National Unity Day commemorating Sardar Patel.

questions.q: The Statue of Unity features a viewing gallery for visitors located at approximately what height?
options: Around 153 metres, at the statue's chest level
At the very feet of the statue
On the head of the statue
questions.explanation: A visitor viewing gallery sits at about 153 metres, near the chest, offering views over the Narmada valley.

questions.q: Roughly how much taller is the Statue of Unity than the Statue of Liberty's figure?
options: Roughly twice the height of the Statue of Liberty's figure
questions.explanation: At 182 metres the statue is roughly double the Statue of Liberty's ground-to-torch height of about 93 metres (which includes its pedestal).

questions.q: Which campaign collected iron from farmers across India as a symbolic contribution to the project?
questions.explanation: An iron-collection campaign gathered farm implements from villages nationwide as a symbolic tribute to the 'Iron Man'.

questions.q: What political office did Sardar Patel hold in independent India's first government?
options: Deputy Prime Minister and Home Minister
questions.explanation: Patel served as the first Deputy Prime Minister and Home Minister of independent India.

questions.q: The area around the Statue of Unity has been developed as a major what?
questions.explanation: The site has been developed into a large tourism complex with gardens, museum and attractions drawing many visitors.

questions.q: The statue is located near which town/tehsil in the Narmada district of Gujarat?
questions.explanation: The statue stands near Kevadia, since renamed Ekta Nagar, in the Narmada district of Gujarat.

questions.q: Which title did the Statue of Unity claim from a previous record-holder upon completion?
options: Tallest statue, surpassing the Spring Temple Buddha
questions.explanation: On completion it overtook China's Spring Temple Buddha (about 128 m) to become the world's tallest statue.

questions.q: Approximately how long did construction of the Statue of Unity take?
questions.explanation: The statue was built in roughly 33 months, an unusually fast pace for a monument of its scale.

questions.q: The Statue of Unity is engineered to withstand strong winds and seismic activity using what core structural system?
options: A reinforced-concrete core with steel framing and bronze cladding
Pure cast bronze with no internal frame
questions.explanation: It uses a reinforced-concrete and steel core supporting an outer bronze cladding, designed to resist high winds and earthquakes.

questions.q: In which key portfolio did Patel personally oversee the accession of princely states after 1947?
questions.explanation: As Home Minister, Patel headed the States Department that negotiated the integration of the princely states.

questions.q: Which posthumous honour, India's highest civilian award, was conferred on Sardar Patel in 1991?
questions.explanation: Sardar Vallabhbhai Patel was awarded the Bharat Ratna posthumously in 1991.

questions.q: The Statue of Unity pedestal/base section is designed to also house which visitor facility?
options: A museum and exhibition hall on Patel's life
questions.explanation: The base of the statue contains a museum, exhibition hall and memorial garden documenting Patel's life and legacy.

questions.q: Roughly how tall is the Statue of Unity compared with the Spring Temple Buddha it surpassed?
questions.explanation: At 182 m versus about 128 m, the Statue of Unity is roughly 54 metres taller than the Spring Temple Buddha.

questions.q: Which engineering challenge most influenced the statue's design near the Narmada River?
options: High wind speeds and seismic stability requirements
questions.explanation: The riverside, open setting required engineering for high wind loads and earthquake resistance for the tall structure.
